enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his Sunday, December 4th 2016 in Boulder — a low-key day of helping his mom move firewood, setting up a Roku box his mom bought him, and spending most of the day working on a Rome-themed project. He mentions listening to a History of Byzantium podcast and feeling enthusiastic about the Rome project.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Still excited and not burned out — Rome looks beautiful, not crowded yet
Losing the sense of beauty in a subject is an early sign of burnout
Finds Byzantium history cool but notes source material online is too scattered and needs organizing

transcript.text: Hey, this is Living in Boulder V-Log for Sunday, December 4th, because today is Monday, December 5th.
I thought that I did the video this morning, dude. I thought that I did the video, but then my girlfriend calls me.
She's like, you didn't do your video. And I'm like, yeah, I'm like, I didn't? Oh my god.
Because what happened was I was working until 6, and then my mom calls me because I was supposed to bring wood from, help her get wood, firewood.
And then she kept calling me because I couldn't get up, and then I went over there at like noon, so I only got like 6 hours of sleep or 1 or something.
And I brought the wood from her car to her house and hung out, and I think I had some food or whatever.
Oh yeah, did some other organizational stuff and then looking for things.
And then came back here and then just working all day on the Rome.
I wanted to figure out the Hulu on my TV.
My mom bought me the Rock Zero or the whatever, the Hulu and the thing, the box.
And, but I don't have the USB cable or the other cable, but to have it work, so I'll buy that tomorrow.
And then I can play videos from my computer on there.
That's what, I'm going to check that out.
That's going to be cool, and maybe even for a micro SD card, because I saw an SD card slot in there, so that'll be chill.
This table's about to wiggle apart.
I need to just tighten it again.
So, yeah, I was just doing random buildings today.
I'm still in the, you know, excitement mode of it, you know.
It hasn't started to get old yet.
You know, I'm still looking at Rome like, oh, Rome is beautiful.
I haven't started looking at Rome and being like, oh God, Rome looks like it would be crowded.
Because if you start looking at something that's not beautiful, then that's the beginning stages of burning out on the project.
But, but yeah, it's going to look cool to go on ground level and travel around.
I've been testing that some.
So, listening to the history of Byzantium.
It's cool, there's a lot of source material on the internet.
It's so disparate, though.
I need to organize it all on my website.
But, yeah, that's pretty interesting.
Byzantium has a pretty interesting history.
So, that's my vlog.
I didn't get, I'm going to get really high right now and have a shower.
I should have gotten real high before the video.
Okay, peace out.
